description:
Science is a clinical stage, vertically integrated technology company focused on solving some of neuroscience's hardest questions and most serious unmet medical needs. We work to restore quality of life to those with debilitating conditions for which there are no treatment options, creating devices aimed at restoring vision, cognition, and mobility to patients who have lost it. To support progress across our industry, we provide state-of-the-art components and vertically integrated infrastructure for others to build on via Science Foundry.

Science is developing a software platform that will touch every part of the company, from external engagement to internal operational and R&D processes. We are looking for experienced web engineers to join onsite in Alameda, CA to help build out this platform, in close collaboration with a world-class team of scientists and engineers. You will work with the microfabrication, biology, animal behavior, surgery, and logistics teams to build tools that they will use every day. Great communication skills are a must, and we're looking for excellent technical and product judgment: can you ask the right questions to absorb other people's mental models, then turn around and build things that make sense for the situation, quickly?

Key Qualifications: 2+ years of hands-on experience as a web software engineer, including significant depth in either backend or fullstack development. Deep understanding of modern web applications design architecture, good API design patterns, and performance. Adaptable and proactive in a fast-paced, startup environment, taking ownership of projects and collaborating effectively. Ability to act as your own product manager and understand the core requirements that make solutions effective. A strong sense of when it's appropriate to address mounting technical debt and when to keep building new features. Preferred Qualifications: Our stack is Rails / React / Typescript / PostgreSQL. Experience with at least one of these technologies is a plus. Experience with app deployment and devops. Salary Range: $100,000-$180,000 + equity + benefits.

Harmony is an open blockchain with data sharding and fast finality. Social games and community AI can use our onchain tokens for micro-payments, smart contracts for market pricing, and zero-knowledge proofs for data privacy. Harmony's mission is to scale trust and create a radically fair economy. Our platform is decentralized, scalable and secure to settle any transactions without trusted parties. Responsibilities: Develop user interfaces for Harmony-based DeFi applications and DApps. Ensure seamless smart contract and wallet integration across platforms. Optimize app performance and implement community feedback. Requirements: Bachelor degree in computer science specializing in kernel, data structures or compilers. 3 to 5 years of working experience including 1 year of startup experience. 60-hour work week, daily 4-hour overlap with Pacific Time. Exceptional communication and people skills.

InComm Payments is a pioneer in the payment (FinTech) industry, founded over 30 years ago with over 3,000 employees in 35 countries. We are looking for a highly skilled Senior Engineer utilizing C#. Responsibilities: As a senior full-stack software engineer, design, develop, build, and implement high availability enterprise applications. Adhere to overall development guidelines and best practices. Qualifications: Minimum 5 years full-stack experience, Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or related field, strong understanding of Agile methodologies, experience with CI/CD, strong experience with .NET Core, C#, SQL, REST API, Microservices, JavaScript, Typescript, HTML, frontend experience with Angular, understanding of cloud native applications (Azure, AWS).

We're searching for a founding engineer who brings a mix of rapid iteration, meticulous craftsmanship, and an eagerness to dive into the fast-evolving world of AI. You'll play a critical role in shaping our technology, from our advanced AI analysis pipeline leveraging cutting-edge models to our robust ingestion system built on Kafka and Clickhouse, down to our web application that users rely on every day. What You'll Do: Design, build, and iterate across the stack, from backend data pipelines and ML-powered insights to frontend applications. Drive the development of our core AI analysis pipeline, working with multi-modal LLMs, modern ML models, and data processing techniques. Work on our ingestion pipeline, handling massive amounts of data at scale with tools like Kafka and Clickhouse. Collaborate closely with the founding team to define technical strategies. About You: 1-3+ years of experience building professional software with a very high quality bar. Expertise in LLMs, machine learning, Next.js, React, Kafka, or Clickhouse. Full-Stack Fluency and AI/ML Enthusiasm.

Adobe is looking for a Machine Learning intern who will apply AI and machine learning techniques to big-data problems to help Adobe better understand, lead and optimize the experience of its customers. You will work on projects like real-time online media optimization, sales operation analytics, customer churn scoring and management, customer understanding, product recommendation and customer lifetime value prediction. What You'll Do: Develop predictive models on large-scale datasets with statistical modeling, machine learning, and analytics techniques. Develop and implement scalable, efficient, and interpretable modeling algorithms for large-scale data in production systems. Collaborate with product management and engineering groups. Requirements: Currently enrolled pursuing a Master's or PhD degree in Computer Science or Computer Engineering with expected graduation December 2026 - June 2027. Good understanding of statistical modeling, machine learning, deep learning, or data analytics. Proficient in Python, Java and C. Familiar with R, Matlab and scikit learn. Pay: $45.00-$55.00 hourly.

Harmony is an open blockchain with data sharding and fast finality. Social games and community AI can use our onchain tokens for micro-payments, smart contracts for market pricing, and zero-knowledge proofs for data privacy. Harmony's mission is to scale trust and create a radically fair economy. Responsibilities: Develop user interfaces for Harmony-based DeFi applications and DApps. Ensure seamless smart contract and wallet integration across platforms. Optimize app performance and implement community feedback. Requirements: Bachelor degree in computer science specializing in kernel, data structures or compilers. 3 to 5 years of working experience including 1 year of startup experience. 60-hour work week, daily 4-hour overlap with Pacific Time. Exceptional communication and people skills.

Twitch is the world's biggest live streaming service. The API Platform team builds high-traffic, internet-facing services that power Twitch web sites, mobile clients and our third party developer community. Our GraphQL edge service is one of the biggest in the industry. The team owns the internal and external messaging platforms that power real-time capabilities, handling billions of notifications per day. You Will: Work with product leaders to build and release new features. Work on high throughput, low latency edge services. Contribute to architectural discussions involving GraphQL API and real-time platforms. Design and deliver robust real-time messaging features. You Have: 1-2 years of experience building distributed, low-latency, high availability applications. A degree in Computer Science or related Engineering discipline. Experience developing with static-typed languages (we work mostly in Go). Bonus: Familiarity with AWS technologies, messaging or event driven systems. Base salary: $127,100-$185,000 USD.

Hipcamp is the #1 app to find and book campsites. At Hipcamp, we're always on the lookout for talented individuals who share our passion for the outdoors. Roles We Typically Hire For: Engineering & Data (Mobile Engineer React Native, Platform Engineer, Full-Stack Engineer, Data Engineer, Data Architect, Engineering Manager), Product & Design (Product Analyst, Product Manager, Product Designer), G&A (Host Onboarding Manager, Growth Marketing Manager, Government & Community Relations Manager, Customer Support Guide, Accountant, Finance Manager). Qualifications: Excited about working in a collaborative, cross-functional environment. Comfort working in fast-paced startup environment. Excellent communication skills. Passion for Hipcamp's mission.

job_title: Full-Stack Software Engineer 5 - Ecosystem and Operational Insights
description: The Client and Partner Technologies (CPT) team builds tools and services to scale the Netflix application platform to over half a billion devices. The Ecosystem and Operational Insights team is looking for a senior full-stack engineer with a front-end focus to help revamp our suite of developer productivity tools. This team accelerates the software developer lifecycle at Netflix by building tools and services that improve feedback loops and enable confident code delivery. What You Will Do: Drive new technical solutions across teams. Design, build, and refine custom developer tooling from the ground up. Revamp existing tools and services. Troubleshoot and resolve issues across the stack. What We Are Looking For: 7+ years of experience in software development. Strong full-stack development skills with emphasis in front-end UI work. Deep experience with React & GraphQL. Strong programming skills in JavaScript, TypeScript, and experience with Java and Node.js. Compensation: $388,000-$558,000.

One of our clients is hiring a Software Engineer to design and enhance Salesforce-based solutions supporting enterprise Quality Management and digital compliance platforms. You will work closely with Engineering, Product, and QA teams to build and optimize Salesforce-based applications. This hybrid role (Hamilton, NJ) includes hands-on Apex development, architectural contributions, CI/CD enablement, and mentorship of junior developers. Key Responsibilities: Design, develop, and maintain applications using Salesforce Apex. Build scalable, reusable components and managed packages (ISV-ready). Develop integrations with backend systems, APIs, and data services. Support CI/CD workflows and automation processes. Ideal Background: Strong experience with Salesforce Apex development, designing scalable enterprise applications, CI/CD pipelines and GitHub, secure software development in regulated environments.

Britive is at the forefront of cloud security with the only modern privileged access management platform providing unified Privileged Access Visibility, Dynamic Privilege Management and Secrets Governance across cloud infrastructures. Key Responsibilities: Design and development of large-scale application stack running on AWS. Collaborate with product management and engineering teams. Perform proof of concepts for best design options. Research integration options for various SaaS/IaaS technologies. Required Skills: Minimum 3 years experience, in-depth knowledge of Spring, Spring Boot, Java, REST Web Services, SQL/NoSQL databases, ELK stack, microservices architecture, Docker, CI/CD. Hands-on experience with scalable multi-tenant SaaS on AWS. Knowledge of AWS networking and security, Route53, API Gateway, ECS/Fargate, RDS, DynamoDB. Fully remote (US only).

DoorDash is building the world's most reliable on-demand logistics engine for delivery. We are looking for Backend Engineers to build and maintain a large scale 24x7 global infrastructure system powering DoorDash's 3-sided marketplace of Consumers, Merchants and Dashers. You Will: Develop, release and run large-scale web applications. Develop and define the backend architecture and tech stack. Improve performance, reliability, scalability and security. Transition monolithic codebase to microservice-based architecture. Requirements: B.S., M.S., or PhD in Computer Science or equivalent. 2+ years industry experience with backend tech stacks. Experience with service oriented architecture, REST APIs, unit testing. Experience with SQL and NoSQL databases (Postgres, Redis, Elasticsearch, RabbitMQ). Base pay: $130,600-$285,000 USD.

job_title: ML Ops Engineer, Machine Learning & AI
description: Machine Learning at the New York Times enhances the experience of 150 million digital readers and grows our subscriber base through content recommendations and personalizations. The ML & AI team builds and maintains infrastructure hosting all real-time ML inference models. Responsibilities: Build and Automate ML Pipelines with robust CI/CD for automated model training, validation, deployment, and retraining. Productionalize Models by packaging, containerizing, and deploying ML models as scalable services. Implement comprehensive monitoring for production models. Manage and evolve MLOps toolchain. Qualifications: 2+ years of software engineering or DevOps experience with focus on MLOps. Experience in Python or Go. Experience with CI/CD pipelines, containerization (Docker, Kubernetes), cloud platforms (AWS, GCP). Base pay: $110,000-$130,000 USD.

DoorDash is looking for Frontend Engineers to build and maintain a large scale 24x7 global infrastructure system. Frontend Engineers help design and build beautiful, intuitive user interfaces for web applications to create the best delivery experience possible. You Will: Become a product owner for client facing applications. Write robust, highly tested, well-crafted code. Fight for the best customer experience through technical and product decisions. Requirements: B.S., M.S. or PhD in relevant field. 2+ years of industry experience. Understanding of cross-browser compatibility, responsive design, accessibility. Experience architecting large-scale frontend applications. Mastery of vanilla JS, HTML5, CSS3. Experience with React/Redux or similar frameworks. Base pay: $130,600-$285,000 USD.

DoorDash is building the world's most reliable on-demand logistics engine for delivery. Looking for Backend Engineers for Product and Product Platform teams. You Will: Develop, release and run large-scale web applications. Develop and define the backend architecture. Improve performance, reliability, scalability and security. Transition monolithic codebase to microservices. Requirements: B.S., M.S., or PhD in Computer Science. 2+ years industry experience with backend tech stacks. Experience with REST APIs, service oriented architecture. Experience with SQL and NoSQL databases. Base pay: $130,600-$285,000 USD.

Common Sense Media is a nonprofit organization giving kids, families, and schools the tools to make informed choices about media and technology. The AI Engineering Intern will work closely with Product, Design, and Engineering teams to prototype AI-powered tools and explore scalable ways of building interactive experiences for students. What You'll Do: Use modern AI development tools (Codex, Claude Code) to rapidly prototype AI-powered features. Build interactive AI experiences for students. Explore scalable frameworks supporting multiple interactive tools. Partner with product managers, designers, and engineers. What We're Looking For: Rising junior, senior, or graduate student in computer science, engineering, AI, or related field. Strong programming skills (Python, JavaScript). Interest in applied AI and rapid prototyping. Duration: June 15-August 21, 2026. Pay: $25/hr.

DoorDash is building the world's most reliable on-demand logistics engine for delivery. Looking for Backend Engineers for Product and Product Platform teams to solve key business problems with elegant technical solutions. You Will: Develop, release and run large-scale web applications. Develop and define the backend architecture. Improve performance, reliability, scalability and security. Transition monolithic codebase to microservices. Requirements: B.S., M.S., or PhD in Computer Science. 2+ years industry experience with backend tech stacks. Experience with REST APIs, service oriented architecture. Experience with SQL and NoSQL databases. Base pay: $130,600-$285,000 USD.

DoorDash is looking for Frontend Engineers to build and maintain a large scale 24x7 global infrastructure system. You Will: Become a product owner for client facing applications. Write robust, highly tested code. Fight for the best customer experience. Requirements: B.S., M.S. or PhD in relevant field. 2+ years industry experience. Understanding of cross-browser compatibility, responsive design, accessibility. Experience architecting large-scale frontend applications. Mastery of vanilla JS, HTML5, CSS3. Experience with React/Redux. Base pay: $130,600-$285,000 USD.

Concord is an AI-first company building the Model Context Protocol (MCP) infrastructure that will make traditional contract management obsolete. We've gained traction with 1,500+ companies and over 1M users. This role is for you if you thrive on building engaging, performant, and scalable user experiences with modern JavaScript frameworks. Tech Stack: JavaScript (ES2020), TypeScript, Ember.js (3.28 - LTS), React (17 - hooks), Functional CSS, CSS-in-JS, REST, AWS, Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK. What We Expect: Developing new features to enhance user experience. Building reusable libraries. Ensuring technical feasibility of designs. Optimizing platform performance. Meeting security, scalability, and high availability requirements. Requirements: At least 5 years experience as a Front End Developer. Proficiency in JavaScript and HTML/CSS. Familiarity with modern JavaScript stacks (React, Ember.js, Vue.js, Angular).

DoorDash is building the world's most reliable on-demand logistics engine for delivery. Looking for Backend Engineers for Product and Product Platform teams. You Will: Develop, release and run large-scale web applications. Develop and define the backend architecture. Improve performance, reliability, scalability and security. Transition monolithic codebase to microservices. Requirements: B.S., M.S., or PhD in Computer Science. 2+ years industry experience with backend tech stacks. Experience with REST APIs, service oriented architecture. Experience with SQL and NoSQL databases. Base pay: $130,600-$285,000 USD.

Britive is at the forefront of cloud security with a modern privileged access management platform. Key Responsibilities: Design and development of large-scale application stack on AWS. Collaborate with product management and engineering teams. Perform proof of concepts. Research integration options for SaaS/IaaS technologies. Required Skills: Minimum 7 years experience, in-depth knowledge of Spring, Spring Boot, Java, REST Web Services, SQL/NoSQL databases, ELK stack, microservices, Docker, CI/CD. Hands-on experience with scalable multi-tenant SaaS on AWS. Knowledge of AWS networking and security, Route53, API Gateway, ECS/Fargate, RDS, DynamoDB. Fully remote (US only).

DoorDash is looking for Frontend Engineers to build and maintain a large scale 24x7 global infrastructure system. You Will: Become a product owner for client facing applications. Write robust, highly tested code. Fight for the best customer experience. Requirements: B.S., M.S. or PhD in relevant field. 2+ years industry experience. Understanding of cross-browser compatibility, responsive design, accessibility. Experience architecting large-scale frontend applications. Mastery of vanilla JS, HTML5, CSS3. Experience with React/Redux. Base pay: $130,600-$285,000 USD.

Grocery is Uber's fastest growing business line. The Shopper Experience team is looking for a strong full-stack web engineer to help build the next generation of fulfillment tools to allow shoppers to quickly and accurately pick and pack items. What You'll Do: Work through the entire software engineering lifecycle. Join on call rotation for continuous improvements on system availability, scalability, performance. Provide technical leadership. Collaborate with infrastructure teams and product managers. Qualifications: Bachelor's or equivalent in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics or 3-years full-time Software Engineering experience, including 2-years in programming languages (C, C++, Java, Python, or Go) and web technologies. Base salary: $171,000-$190,000.

job_title: Solution Engineering - Manager/Sr Manager, Director, Senior Director
description: Salesforce is the #1 AI CRM. Come join the Solution Engineering leadership org. Through collaboration with customers, Product teams, and Professional Services, Solutions Engineers develop thoughtful solutions that help customers connect with their customers in new ways. Your Mission: Lead transformation efforts, inspire and develop high-performing teams, collaborate with Sales leadership, shape solutions, foster growth and innovation. Key Responsibilities: Partner with Sales leadership, drive mindset shift, lead diverse Solutions Engineering team, attract and retain top talent. Skills: 10+ years leadership experience in strategy, management consulting, or sales organizations. Strong understanding of cloud computing technologies. Base salary: $155,190-$342,440.

Savance is a fast-paced software development company. We are looking for a talented junior to mid-level software engineer who loves to solve complex problems. Job Responsibilities: Actively participating in the software development lifecycle. Research software issues. Create plans to address software defects. Gather requirements and work with team members and customers. Work on multiple projects while maintaining priorities. Qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Engineering/Computer Science/IT. 3+ years experience with C#, WinForms or WPF, OOP and multithreading. 3+ years with Microsoft SQL Server. 3+ years with ASP.Net, Angular, Web API, Restful web services. 3+ years GIT and Azure DevOps. Salary: $60,000-$80,000. Remote, work from home position.

Docusign brings agreements to life with over 1.5 million customers in over 180 countries. We are building a cutting-edge App ecosystem that will empower developers to seamlessly integrate and extend Docusign's features through custom APIs. Responsibilities: Contribute to development and maintenance of Docusign's App ecosystem. Implement solutions using Azure technologies, Kubernetes, Terraform. Assist in designing secure and scalable systems. Participate in code reviews. Collaborate on internal processes. Requirements: 5+ years professional software development experience. Experience with NodeJS/Typescript. Experience with Azure services, CI/CD pipelines, Kubernetes, Terraform. Experience with queueing systems, NoSQL databases, SQL databases. Hybrid role, minimum 2 days per week in office. Base salary: $133,800-$197,750.

The $6 trillion chemical industry runs the physical economy but still relies on email and Excel. We are bringing agentic AI to the industry, starting with quoting and pricing tools. We are deployed in major manufacturers and distributors, including Fortune 100 companies. In Your First 30 Days: Visit a customer plant, build an end-to-end agent for complex quoting/pricing, plan and build an ERP or CRM integration, prototype new product features. What we look for: Strong full stack ability. You'll be part UI designer, product manager, data engineer, and prompt whisperer. Based in San Francisco or willing to relocate. Stack: Typescript, Next, React. We use LLMs from OpenAI, Gemini, and Anthropic.

DoorDash is building the world's most reliable on-demand logistics engine for delivery. Looking for Backend Engineers for Product and Product Platform teams. You Will: Develop, release and run large-scale web applications. Develop and define the backend architecture. Improve performance, reliability, scalability and security. Transition monolithic codebase to microservices. Requirements: B.S., M.S., or PhD in Computer Science. 2+ years industry experience with backend tech stacks. Experience with REST APIs, service oriented architecture. Experience with SQL and NoSQL databases. Base pay: $130,600-$285,000 USD.

Clay's mission is to help organizations turn any growth idea into reality. We crossed $100M in revenue and raised a $100M Series C at a $5B valuation. We're looking for a Full Stack Engineer responsible for building products that help self-serve and enterprise customers translate growth ideas into reality. What You'll Do: Drive product strategy and execution. Design and build full stack features. Up-level the codebase (negative maintenance is a core value). Mentor and learn from other engineers. What You'll Bring: 4+ years hands-on engineering experience. Love being a product manager as well as an engineer. Empathetic communicator. Tech stack: React, Typescript, Python, Node.js, AWS services (Aurora Postgres, Elasticache Redis, ECS Fargate, Lambda, OpenSearch), Terraform. Compensation: $130K-$300K.

job_title: AI Engineer Intern - AI/ML/GenAI Design & Development
description: PM Accelerator is a US based company premiering in AI learning and development, featuring award-winning AI products and mentors from Google, Meta, Apple, and Nvidia. Role Overview: Seeking AI Engineering Interns for Spring & Summer 2026. You will work on projects involving large language models, machine learning, prompt engineering, GenAI application development, ChatGPT API integration, and data pipeline development. Requirements: Front-end skills in React.JS, back-end in Python, data science in modeling and data pipelines. Key Responsibilities: Collaborate to design and develop AI-driven applications. Explore GenAI tools and frameworks. Execute integration of AI technologies including LLMs, ML models, and APIs. Contribute to data pipelines. This is an unpaid internship with CPT and OPT sponsorship available. Duration: 3 months (April-June or June-August 2026).

As an entry-level Data Scientist in our Chief Data & Analytics Office, you will work on agile teams with experienced data scientists to develop machine learning models that drive business decisions across consumer banking and commercial lending. Responsibilities include collecting, cleaning, and preprocessing structured and unstructured data from multiple sources; performing exploratory data analysis to identify trends and patterns in financial data; building predictive models and machine learning algorithms using Python and R; developing data pipelines for model training and evaluation; creating data visualizations and dashboards using Tableau to communicate findings to non-technical stakeholders; applying statistical methods including hypothesis testing, regression analysis, and A/B testing to evaluate business strategies; collaborating with engineering teams to deploy models into production environments; and documenting methodologies and presenting results to senior leadership. Required qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or related STEM field; proficiency in Python and SQL; experience with data manipulation libraries such as pandas, NumPy, and scikit-learn; understanding of statistical concepts including probability distributions, hypothesis testing, and regression; familiarity with machine learning techniques including classification, regression, clustering, and dimensionality reduction; strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Preferred qualifications: experience with deep learning frameworks such as TensorFlow or PyTorch; knowledge of big data technologies like Spark or Hadoop; experience with cloud platforms such as AWS or Azure; familiarity with version control using Git.

Nationwide is seeking a Junior Data Scientist to join our Advanced Analytics team. You will apply data science techniques to solve complex insurance and financial services problems. Responsibilities include developing and validating predictive models for claims forecasting, customer segmentation, and risk assessment; performing data wrangling and feature engineering on large-scale datasets; building classification and regression models to predict customer behavior and policy outcomes; conducting statistical analysis to support pricing and underwriting decisions; creating automated reports and interactive dashboards using Python and visualization tools; collaborating with actuaries, underwriters, and business analysts to translate business requirements into analytical solutions; applying natural language processing techniques to analyze customer feedback and claims narratives; and maintaining model performance through monitoring and retraining. Requirements: Bachelor's or Master's degree in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Science, Mathematics, or a quantitative field; strong programming skills in Python including pandas, scikit-learn, and matplotlib; proficiency in SQL for data extraction and manipulation; solid foundation in statistics including linear regression, logistic regression, decision trees, and ensemble methods; experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau or Power BI; understanding of the machine learning model lifecycle from data preparation to deployment; excellent communication skills and ability to present technical findings to business stakeholders; experience with Jupyter notebooks or similar interactive development environments.

Root Insurance is transforming auto insurance using data science and telematics. As a Data Scientist I, you will work on our core modeling team to develop and improve the algorithms that power our mobile-first insurance platform. Responsibilities include building machine learning models that analyze driving behavior data from mobile sensors to assess risk; developing feature engineering pipelines that extract meaningful signals from time-series telematics data; applying statistical modeling techniques to price insurance policies accurately and fairly; conducting A/B tests to evaluate the impact of model changes on business metrics; working with large-scale datasets using SQL, Python, and distributed computing frameworks; implementing and evaluating classification, regression, and clustering algorithms; collaborating with mobile engineers to understand data collection from iOS and Android sensors including accelerometer, gyroscope, and GPS; building dashboards and data visualizations to monitor model performance and business KPIs; contributing to our experimentation platform and causal inference methods. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Physics, or related quantitative field; strong Python programming skills with experience in data science libraries; proficiency in SQL and experience working with relational databases; solid understanding of probability, statistics, and machine learning algorithms; familiarity with time-series analysis and signal processing; experience with version control systems like Git; strong problem-solving and analytical reasoning skills. Nice to have: experience with mobile sensor data or IoT applications; knowledge of Bayesian methods; experience with causal inference techniques; familiarity with cloud infrastructure such as AWS.

At Capital One, data is at the center of everything we do. As an Associate Data Scientist, you will apply machine learning and statistical modeling to solve problems in fraud detection, credit risk, and customer experience. Responsibilities include developing and implementing machine learning models for real-time fraud detection and credit decisioning; analyzing large, complex datasets to uncover patterns and insights that drive strategic business decisions; building end-to-end data pipelines including data extraction, transformation, feature engineering, model training, and deployment; designing and analyzing A/B tests and experiments to measure the effectiveness of new products and features; applying advanced techniques including gradient boosting, neural networks, and natural language processing; collaborating with cross-functional teams including product managers, software engineers, and business analysts; communicating findings and recommendations to technical and non-technical audiences through visualizations and presentations; contributing to our open-source tools and internal data science platform. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Engineering, or a quantitative discipline; programming experience in Python, including libraries like pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn, and TensorFlow or PyTorch; SQL proficiency and experience with large-scale data processing; strong foundation in statistics, linear algebra, and probability theory; knowledge of machine learning algorithms including supervised and unsupervised learning, ensemble methods, and deep learning; familiarity with software engineering best practices including version control, testing, and code review; strong communication and collaboration skills.

Amazon's Data Science team works on some of the most complex challenges in retail, logistics, and cloud computing. As an entry-level Data Scientist, you will develop models and analytics that directly impact customer experience and operational efficiency. Responsibilities include building machine learning models to optimize supply chain operations, demand forecasting, and product recommendations; designing and implementing scalable data pipelines using AWS services including S3, Redshift, and SageMaker; performing causal analysis to measure the impact of business initiatives and product launches; applying statistical modeling, machine learning, and deep learning to solve ambiguous business problems; developing A/B testing frameworks and analyzing experimental results with statistical rigor; creating automated dashboards and reports to track key business metrics; writing production-quality code in Python and SQL to support data-driven decision making; collaborating with product managers, engineers, and business leaders to define problem statements and deliver insights. Requirements: Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Operations Research, or related field; strong programming skills in Python with experience in scientific computing and ML libraries; expert-level SQL skills; deep understanding of statistics including experimental design, causal inference, and Bayesian methods; experience with machine learning including classification, regression, clustering, natural language processing, and recommendation systems; familiarity with distributed computing concepts and big data tools; ability to communicate complex analyses clearly to diverse audiences; bias for action and comfort with ambiguity. Preferred: experience with AWS cloud services; knowledge of deep learning frameworks; publications or projects in machine learning or statistics.

CoverMyMeds, a McKesson company, is the leader in electronic prior authorization technology. As a Junior Data Scientist on our Analytics team, you will leverage healthcare data to improve medication access for patients. Responsibilities include analyzing healthcare claims data and electronic health records to identify trends in medication access and prior authorization outcomes; building predictive models to forecast prior authorization approval rates and processing times; developing natural language processing models to extract structured information from unstructured clinical documents; creating interactive dashboards and data visualizations using Python, R, and business intelligence tools; performing cohort analysis and statistical testing to evaluate the impact of product features on patient outcomes; collaborating with product teams, pharmacists, and healthcare experts to understand clinical workflows; applying machine learning techniques including text classification, named entity recognition, and clustering to healthcare data; building and maintaining ETL pipelines for data processing and model training. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Biostatistics, or related quantitative field; proficiency in Python or R for data analysis and modeling; SQL skills for querying relational databases; understanding of statistical methods including regression, classification, and hypothesis testing; experience with data visualization libraries such as matplotlib, seaborn, or plotly; familiarity with natural language processing concepts and libraries like NLTK or spaCy; strong analytical and problem-solving abilities; excellent written and verbal communication skills. Preferred: experience with healthcare data or clinical informatics; knowledge of HIPAA data privacy requirements; experience with cloud-based analytics platforms.

Google's Data Science team applies quantitative analysis, data mining, and machine learning to turn data into insights that drive product decisions. As a New Graduate Data Scientist, you will work with product and engineering teams to design experiments, build models, and analyze data at massive scale. Responsibilities include defining metrics and goals for products; designing, implementing, and analyzing large-scale experiments and A/B tests; applying machine learning, statistics, and optimization to improve products used by billions of people; performing statistical analysis on large datasets to identify opportunities and diagnose issues; building dashboards, reports, and data pipelines to support ongoing product measurement; developing predictive and prescriptive models to inform product strategy; collaborating with software engineers, UX researchers, and product managers; presenting findings to senior leadership and influencing product roadmap decisions. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Computer Science, Applied Mathematics, Operations Research, Economics, or related quantitative field; experience with statistical software such as R or Python and database languages such as SQL; applied experience with machine learning on large datasets; strong knowledge of statistics including experimental design, regression analysis, and time-series analysis; familiarity with programming languages such as Python, Java, or C++; experience articulating and translating business questions into analyses; demonstrated analytical and quantitative problem-solving ability; strong communication skills. Preferred: Master's or PhD in a quantitative discipline; experience with big data processing frameworks like MapReduce or Spark; experience with information retrieval, natural language processing, or recommendation systems.

Spotify is looking for a Data Scientist to help us understand how millions of listeners interact with music and podcasts. You will use data to shape product strategy and improve user engagement. Responsibilities include analyzing user behavior data to generate insights about listening patterns, content discovery, and engagement; building recommendation models and algorithms that personalize the listening experience; designing and analyzing A/B tests to evaluate new features and product changes; developing machine learning models for content classification, user segmentation, and churn prediction; creating statistical models to understand and predict key business metrics; building data pipelines and ETL processes to enable self-service analytics; partnering with product managers, designers, and engineers to define metrics and success criteria for new features; communicating insights through data storytelling using visualizations and presentations. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Engineering, or related field; strong proficiency in Python and SQL; experience with data analysis and machine learning libraries including pandas, scikit-learn, and TensorFlow; solid understanding of statistical inference, hypothesis testing, and experimental design; experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau, Looker, or matplotlib; understanding of recommendation systems and collaborative filtering; familiarity with distributed data processing tools like Spark or BigQuery; strong communication skills and ability to influence product decisions through data. Preferred: experience with large-scale user behavior data; knowledge of audio signal processing or music information retrieval; experience with deep learning for NLP or recommendation systems.

Battelle is the world's largest nonprofit research and development organization. As a Junior Data Scientist, you will apply advanced analytics and machine learning to solve national security, health, and energy challenges. Responsibilities include developing and implementing machine learning and statistical models for defense, health, and environmental applications; analyzing satellite imagery, sensor data, and geospatial datasets using computer vision and deep learning techniques; building classification and object detection models for image analysis and pattern recognition; processing and analyzing large-scale scientific datasets including time-series, spatial, and graph data; applying natural language processing to extract insights from technical documents and reports; developing data visualization dashboards to communicate findings to government and research stakeholders; collaborating with multidisciplinary teams of scientists, engineers, and domain experts; writing technical reports and presenting results at project reviews. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Mathematics, Physics, or related STEM field; programming experience in Python with scientific computing libraries including NumPy, SciPy, and scikit-learn; experience with deep learning frameworks such as TensorFlow or PyTorch; knowledge of computer vision techniques and image processing; proficiency in SQL; understanding of statistical analysis and experimental design; strong analytical and critical thinking skills; ability to obtain and maintain a U.S. security clearance. Preferred: experience with geospatial data analysis or GIS tools; knowledge of reinforcement learning or optimization methods; experience with containerization tools like Docker; familiarity with Linux environments and high-performance computing.

Meta is seeking Data Scientists to work on products that connect billions of people. You will use data analysis and machine learning to understand user behavior, inform product development, and optimize Meta's family of apps. Responsibilities include applying technical expertise in quantitative analysis, data mining, and visualization to see beyond the numbers and understand how users interact with our products; partnering with product and engineering teams to solve problems, identify trends and opportunities, and measure the impact of initiatives; building and maintaining data pipelines and dashboards for ongoing product monitoring; designing and evaluating experiments to understand causal effects of product changes; developing machine learning models for content ranking, user growth, and engagement optimization; performing exploratory data analysis at scale using SQL and Python; defining key metrics and building analytical frameworks for products at various stages of development; informing and influencing product roadmaps by providing data-driven recommendations. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Physics, Engineering, or Economics; proficiency in SQL and at least one programming language such as Python or R; experience with applied statistics including regression, classification, and experimental design; demonstrated ability to apply technical skills to answer business questions; strong communication skills with the ability to synthesize and present analysis to diverse audiences; self-starter mentality and ability to manage multiple projects in a fast-paced environment. Preferred: Master's or PhD in a quantitative field; experience with large-scale data processing systems; experience with machine learning and deep learning; experience with data visualization tools.

Databricks is the data and AI company, founded by the original creators of Apache Spark. As a New Grad Data Scientist, you will work with our customers and internal teams to develop cutting-edge analytics and machine learning solutions. Responsibilities include building and optimizing machine learning models for diverse applications using Spark and MLflow; developing scalable data pipelines for large-scale data processing on the Databricks Lakehouse platform; applying advanced statistical methods and machine learning techniques to solve complex business problems; working with distributed computing frameworks to process and analyze petabyte-scale datasets; implementing feature engineering and model selection pipelines; designing and analyzing experiments to measure model and product performance; creating comprehensive data visualizations and automated reporting solutions; collaborating with product, engineering, and solutions teams to deliver data-driven insights. Requirements: Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Data Science, or related quantitative field; strong proficiency in Python and experience with data science libraries like pandas, scikit-learn, and PySpark; solid SQL skills and understanding of data modeling concepts; experience with distributed computing and big data frameworks such as Apache Spark; knowledge of machine learning algorithms including classification, regression, clustering, and deep learning; understanding of software engineering practices including Git, CI/CD, and testing; strong analytical thinking and communication skills. Preferred: experience with MLOps and model deployment; familiarity with cloud platforms like AWS, Azure, or GCP; experience with Delta Lake or Apache Spark internals; contributions to open-source projects.

Honda R&D Americas is looking for an Associate Data Scientist to join our Connected and Intelligent Vehicles division. You will apply data science to improve vehicle safety, autonomous driving, and connected car experiences. Responsibilities include developing machine learning models for sensor fusion, object detection, and autonomous driving applications; analyzing large-scale driving datasets including camera, LiDAR, radar, and GPS data; building computer vision models for real-time object classification and tracking; applying time-series analysis and signal processing to vehicle telemetry data; developing simulation environments and synthetic data generation pipelines for model training; implementing and evaluating deep learning architectures including CNNs, RNNs, and transformers for automotive applications; collaborating with mechanical, electrical, and software engineers on vehicle integration; analyzing real-world driving scenarios to improve safety systems and driver assistance features. Requirements: Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Data Science, Robotics, or related field; strong Python programming skills with experience in NumPy, pandas, and deep learning frameworks; knowledge of computer vision techniques and libraries like OpenCV; understanding of deep learning architectures including convolutional and recurrent neural networks; experience with data processing and analysis at scale; familiarity with linear algebra, calculus, and optimization methods; strong problem-solving and analytical skills. Preferred: experience with autonomous vehicle perception or sensor fusion; familiarity with ROS or automotive simulation tools; experience with embedded systems or edge computing; knowledge of C++ for performance-critical applications.

Olive AI builds intelligent automation solutions for healthcare. As a Data Scientist I, you will develop AI models that automate repetitive healthcare processes and improve operational efficiency. Responsibilities include building machine learning models for document classification, information extraction, and process automation in healthcare settings; applying natural language processing to analyze clinical documents, insurance claims, and medical records; developing computer vision models for document understanding including OCR enhancement, layout detection, and form field extraction; creating and optimizing data pipelines for training and evaluating machine learning models; implementing active learning and human-in-the-loop systems to continuously improve model performance; analyzing model metrics and conducting error analysis to identify improvement opportunities; collaborating with product managers and engineers to integrate ML models into production automation workflows; contributing to the development of our AI platform and shared machine learning infrastructure. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Machine Learning, or related field; proficiency in Python and experience with ML frameworks such as PyTorch or TensorFlow; experience with NLP libraries and techniques including text classification, named entity recognition, and transformer models; understanding of computer vision and deep learning for document analysis; SQL proficiency for data querying and analysis; familiarity with software engineering practices including version control, code review, and automated testing; strong analytical and problem-solving skills; excellent communication and teamwork abilities. Preferred: experience with healthcare data or clinical NLP; knowledge of MLOps and model deployment practices; experience with cloud services such as AWS or GCP; familiarity with containerization using Docker or Kubernetes.

Bath & Body Works is seeking a Junior Data Scientist to join our Business Analytics team. You will use data to understand customer behavior, optimize marketing strategies, and drive sales growth. Responsibilities include building customer segmentation and lifetime value models using transactional and behavioral data; developing predictive models for demand forecasting, inventory optimization, and sales projections; analyzing A/B test results for marketing campaigns, promotions, and pricing strategies; creating customer propensity models to improve targeting for email, digital advertising, and loyalty programs; performing market basket analysis and association rule mining to optimize product recommendations and store layouts; building automated reporting pipelines and interactive dashboards for business stakeholders; applying time-series forecasting methods to predict seasonal trends and promotional impacts; collaborating with marketing, merchandising, and e-commerce teams to translate analytical insights into business actions. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Data Science, Computer Science, Marketing Analytics, or related quantitative field; proficiency in Python or R for data analysis and statistical modeling; strong SQL skills for working with large transactional databases; understanding of statistical methods including regression, classification, and time-series analysis; experience with data visualization using Tableau, Power BI, or similar tools; knowledge of customer analytics concepts such as RFM analysis, cohort analysis, and churn modeling; strong business acumen and ability to communicate data-driven insights; excellent problem-solving and organizational skills. Preferred: experience with retail or e-commerce analytics; familiarity with marketing technology platforms; experience with cloud data warehouses like Snowflake or BigQuery; knowledge of A/B testing and experimental design.

TikTok is the leading destination for short-form mobile video. As a New Grad Data Scientist on our Product Analytics team, you will use data to understand content ecosystems, user engagement, and growth patterns across one of the world's fastest-growing platforms. Responsibilities include performing in-depth analyses of user behavior, content trends, and platform health metrics at massive scale; building machine learning models for content recommendation, user growth, and engagement prediction; designing and analyzing large-scale A/B experiments to evaluate product changes and algorithm updates; developing statistical models to understand causal relationships and long-term effects of product decisions; creating automated dashboards and monitoring systems for real-time product metrics; applying NLP techniques to analyze user-generated content and understand content quality; collaborating with product managers, engineers, and content moderators to inform product strategy; working with recommendation system engineers to evaluate and improve content ranking algorithms. Requirements: Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, or related quantitative field; strong proficiency in Python and SQL; experience with data analysis libraries such as pandas, NumPy, and scikit-learn; solid understanding of statistics including hypothesis testing, regression, causal inference, and Bayesian methods; experience with machine learning techniques including classification, regression, and recommendation systems; familiarity with big data processing tools such as Hive, Spark, or Presto; strong analytical thinking and communication skills; ability to work in a fast-paced, cross-functional environment. Preferred: experience with user behavior analysis at scale; knowledge of recommendation systems and information retrieval; experience with deep learning or NLP; familiarity with data pipeline orchestration tools.

Google's Associate Product Manager program is designed for new graduates who want to build products used by billions of people. As an APM, you will define product strategy, work with engineering teams, and ship features that improve user experience across Google's product portfolio. Responsibilities include defining product requirements and writing detailed product specification documents; conducting user research through interviews, surveys, and usability studies to understand user needs and pain points; analyzing product metrics and user behavior data using SQL and analytics tools to inform product decisions; collaborating with software engineers, UX designers, and data scientists to develop and launch new features; prioritizing features and managing the product backlog using agile methodologies; designing and analyzing A/B experiments to measure the impact of product changes; conducting competitive analysis and market research to identify opportunities and threats; presenting product strategy and progress updates to senior leadership and cross-functional stakeholders; working with marketing and sales teams to develop go-to-market strategies. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business, or related field; strong analytical skills with the ability to use data to drive decisions; experience with SQL and data analysis tools; excellent written and verbal communication skills; ability to work effectively with engineering teams and understand technical trade-offs; demonstrated leadership experience through extracurricular activities, internships, or projects; strong problem-solving and critical thinking abilities; passion for technology and building products that improve people's lives. Preferred: experience with product management tools such as JIRA, Asana, or Figma; previous internship experience in product management, engineering, or consulting; understanding of UX design principles and user-centered design processes; familiarity with agile and scrum development methodologies.

Meta's Rotational Product Manager program provides new graduates with the opportunity to build products that connect people across Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and emerging platforms. As a Rotational PM, you will rotate through different product teams, gaining broad exposure to consumer and enterprise products. Responsibilities include identifying product opportunities by analyzing user behavior data, market trends, and competitive landscape; writing product requirements documents and user stories that clearly articulate feature specifications; collaborating with engineering and design teams to build and iterate on product features; defining key metrics and success criteria for product launches; conducting user research including interviews, focus groups, and surveys to validate product hypotheses; analyzing large-scale data using SQL, Excel, and analytics platforms to extract insights and measure product performance; managing the product development lifecycle from ideation through launch and post-launch optimization; working with cross-functional teams including marketing, policy, legal, and data science; presenting product strategy and results to executive leadership. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in any field with strong analytical and technical aptitude; experience with data analysis and comfort working with SQL and spreadsheets; excellent communication skills with the ability to articulate complex ideas clearly; demonstrated leadership and ability to influence without authority; strong user empathy and ability to understand diverse user needs; interest in social technology and connecting communities; ability to balance multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment; creative problem-solving and willingness to challenge assumptions. Preferred: technical background in computer science or engineering; experience with A/B testing and experimental design; prior product management or UX research experience; understanding of mobile app development and platform dynamics.

Capital One's Product Development Program gives new graduates hands-on product management experience in financial technology. You will drive product strategy for digital banking products used by millions of customers. Responsibilities include owning features within Capital One's mobile banking app and web platform; conducting market research and competitive analysis to identify opportunities in digital banking; writing user stories and acceptance criteria for engineering teams; working with UX designers to create wireframes, prototypes, and user flows based on customer research; analyzing product metrics including conversion rates, engagement metrics, and customer satisfaction scores; managing sprints and the agile development process using JIRA and Confluence; conducting A/B tests to optimize user experience and conversion funnels; partnering with data science teams to integrate machine learning features into products; understanding regulatory requirements and ensuring product compliance; presenting product roadmap and business cases to stakeholders. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Business, Computer Science, Engineering, Economics, or related field; strong quantitative and analytical skills; proficiency in SQL and data analysis; excellent communication and presentation skills; ability to translate customer needs into product requirements; understanding of agile development methodologies; strong project management and organizational skills; curiosity about financial services and fintech innovation. Preferred: experience with wireframing or prototyping tools like Figma or Sketch; familiarity with product analytics tools such as Google Analytics, Amplitude, or Mixpanel; prior internship in product management, consulting, or technology; understanding of banking and financial services products.

Microsoft is looking for a Product Manager I to join one of our product teams working on Azure, Microsoft 365, or developer tools. You will help define the future of cloud computing and productivity software used by hundreds of millions of people. Responsibilities include defining product vision and strategy for your area of ownership within a larger product suite; gathering and analyzing customer feedback from enterprise and consumer users to identify unmet needs; writing detailed product specifications, feature briefs, and technical design documents; working closely with software engineers to scope technical requirements and make trade-off decisions; defining key performance indicators and building dashboards to track product health and adoption; conducting competitive analysis across the technology landscape; managing the product backlog and sprint planning using agile practices; coordinating with partner teams across Microsoft to ensure platform consistency and integration; driving cross-functional alignment with engineering, design, marketing, and support teams; understanding and incorporating accessibility requirements and inclusive design principles. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business, Information Systems, or related field; technical aptitude with the ability to understand and discuss software architecture and APIs; strong data analysis skills including SQL and Excel; excellent written and verbal communication skills; experience working on technical projects either through coursework, internships, or personal projects; demonstrated ability to collaborate across disciplines and influence stakeholders; passion for cloud computing, developer tools, or enterprise software; growth mindset and willingness to learn. Preferred: experience with cloud platforms such as Azure, AWS, or GCP; familiarity with software development practices and CI/CD pipelines; prior experience with product management or program management; understanding of enterprise customer needs and B2B sales cycles.

Duolingo is the world's most popular language learning platform. As an Associate Product Manager, you will help build features that make learning accessible, effective, and fun for hundreds of millions of learners worldwide. Responsibilities include leading a specific feature area of the Duolingo app, seeing it through from ideation to launch; conducting user research including interviews, user testing sessions, and analysis of learner behavior data; defining and tracking product metrics such as daily active users, lesson completion rates, and learner retention; collaborating with engineers, designers, learning scientists, and content creators to build engaging educational experiences; analyzing large datasets using SQL and analytics tools to understand how product changes affect learning outcomes; designing A/B experiments to test hypotheses about user engagement and pedagogical effectiveness; writing clear product requirements documents and communicating product decisions to the team; managing the development process through sprint planning and daily standups using agile methodologies; researching competitive landscape and emerging trends in edtech and gamification. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Design, Economics, or related field; strong analytical skills and experience with data-driven decision making; proficiency in SQL for data analysis; excellent communication and collaboration skills; passion for education and language learning; ability to balance user experience with business goals and educational outcomes; experience with product development through coursework, internships, or side projects; creative problem-solving and comfort with ambiguity. Preferred: experience with A/B testing and statistical significance; familiarity with mobile app design patterns and user interface principles; understanding of gamification principles and behavioral psychology; experience with product management or UX research tools.

CoverMyMeds, a McKesson company, is the leader in electronic prior authorization. As a Junior Product Manager, you will help build technology that improves medication access for patients and reduces administrative burden for healthcare providers. Responsibilities include working with clinical and engineering teams to define product features for our electronic prior authorization platform; conducting user research with healthcare providers, pharmacists, and patients to understand pain points in the medication access journey; analyzing product usage data and healthcare claims data to identify opportunities for improvement; writing user stories and product requirements for engineering teams; managing feature development through agile sprints using JIRA; collaborating with UX designers to create intuitive user interfaces for complex healthcare workflows; defining success metrics and analyzing product performance after launch; coordinating with regulatory and compliance teams to ensure healthcare data privacy requirements are met; participating in customer demos and gathering feedback for product iterations; understanding the healthcare technology ecosystem including EHR systems, pharmacy platforms, and insurance processes. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Business, Health Informatics, or related field; strong analytical and problem-solving skills; ability to analyze data using SQL or Excel; excellent written and verbal communication skills; ability to understand and communicate technical concepts; interest in healthcare technology and improving patient outcomes; organizational skills and ability to manage multiple workstreams; collaborative mindset and ability to work with diverse stakeholders. Preferred: experience with healthcare IT or health informatics; familiarity with agile development methodologies; experience with wireframing or prototyping tools; understanding of healthcare regulations such as HIPAA.

Atlassian builds collaboration and productivity tools including Jira, Confluence, and Trello used by millions of teams worldwide. As a New Grad Product Manager, you will own features that help software teams plan, track, and ship work more effectively. Responsibilities include defining product strategy for features within Atlassian's project management and collaboration tools; conducting user research with software development teams, project managers, and business users; analyzing product usage patterns and customer feedback to prioritize feature development; writing product requirements and working with engineering teams to scope technical solutions; collaborating with UX designers to create user-centered designs for complex workflow tools; defining and tracking metrics including feature adoption, user engagement, and customer satisfaction; managing the product development lifecycle using agile practices with Jira; designing experiments to test new features and validate product hypotheses; working with partner ecosystem teams to understand integration requirements and API needs; contributing to go-to-market planning for feature launches. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business, Design, or related field; analytical mindset with experience using data to make decisions; proficiency in SQL and comfort working with product analytics tools; strong written and verbal communication skills; understanding of software development processes and agile methodologies; ability to work collaboratively with engineering and design teams; interest in developer tools and productivity software; demonstrated leadership and initiative through projects or extracurriculars. Preferred: experience with project management tools and collaboration software; understanding of APIs and software integrations; experience with product discovery techniques and user research methods; familiarity with SaaS business models and product-led growth strategies.

Salesforce's APM Futureforce program trains the next generation of product leaders. You will work on our CRM platform, AI features, or industry-specific cloud products that help businesses connect with their customers. Responsibilities include supporting the development of product features for Salesforce's enterprise platform; conducting market research and competitive analysis in the CRM and enterprise software space; gathering requirements from customers, sales teams, and customer success managers; writing product specifications and user stories for engineering teams; collaborating with UX designers to create enterprise-grade user experiences; analyzing adoption metrics, customer usage patterns, and satisfaction data; managing feature development using agile methodologies; participating in customer advisory boards and gathering direct feedback; working with the platform team to understand technical architecture and extensibility requirements; contributing to product documentation and training materials. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Business, Engineering, or related field; strong analytical and quantitative skills; proficiency in SQL and data analysis; excellent communication skills with the ability to present to diverse audiences; understanding of business processes and enterprise software concepts; ability to work with large, cross-functional teams; interest in cloud computing, AI, and enterprise technology; strong organizational and project management skills. Preferred: experience with Salesforce products or CRM systems; understanding of API design and platform ecosystem concepts; experience with enterprise customer interactions; familiarity with AI and machine learning concepts and their business applications.

Amazon is seeking entry-level Product Managers to drive innovation across retail, AWS, Alexa, and other business lines. You will work backwards from the customer to define and deliver products that delight users. Responsibilities include writing detailed product requirement documents starting from the customer experience and working backwards; defining product vision and strategy aligned with Amazon's leadership principles; analyzing customer data, market trends, and business metrics to identify opportunities; collaborating with engineering teams to define technical requirements and make trade-off decisions; managing the product lifecycle from conception through launch and continuous improvement; designing and analyzing experiments to validate product hypotheses and measure impact; creating business cases including revenue projections, cost analysis, and return on investment; working with UX designers to develop customer-centered experiences; partnering with operations, marketing, and business development teams; defining key metrics and building mechanisms to track product performance. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business, Economics, or related field; strong analytical skills with the ability to use data to drive decisions; proficiency in SQL and experience with data analysis; excellent written communication skills including the ability to write clear narratives and documents; customer obsession and ability to think from the customer's perspective; bias for action and comfort with making decisions under uncertainty; ability to work cross-functionally and influence without direct authority; strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail. Preferred: technical background with understanding of software development; experience with product management tools and analytics platforms; MBA or advanced degree; experience in retail, e-commerce, or cloud computing.

Intuit's Rotational Product Manager program develops emerging product leaders who will shape the future of TurboTax, QuickBooks, Credit Karma, and Mailchimp. As an Associate PM, you will solve real financial problems for consumers and small businesses. Responsibilities include conducting deep customer research to understand financial pain points and unmet needs; defining product features that simplify complex financial tasks for small business owners and consumers; analyzing product metrics and user behavior data to optimize product experiences; collaborating with machine learning and data science teams to integrate AI-powered features; writing product specifications and managing feature development through agile sprints; designing and evaluating A/B tests to improve conversion, engagement, and retention metrics; working with design teams to create intuitive interfaces for financial products; understanding tax, accounting, and personal finance domains to build domain-appropriate solutions; partnering with marketing teams to develop product positioning and messaging; contributing to long-term product roadmap and strategic planning. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Business, Design, Economics, or related field; strong quantitative and analytical skills; comfort with data analysis using SQL and spreadsheets; excellent written and verbal communication skills; empathy for users and ability to translate needs into product solutions; understanding of agile product development; interest in financial technology and democratizing financial services; ability to collaborate effectively with engineering and design teams. Preferred: experience with consumer financial products or fintech; understanding of AI and machine learning concepts; experience with user research methods; familiarity with product analytics tools such as Amplitude, Mixpanel, or Google Analytics.

Root Insurance is reimagining auto insurance through technology. As a Junior Product Manager, you will help build and improve our mobile-first insurance platform that uses data science to offer fairer pricing. Responsibilities include owning features within Root's mobile app and web platform; conducting user research to understand how customers interact with insurance products; analyzing user behavior data to identify friction points in the quoting, purchasing, and claims processes; writing product requirements and user stories for engineering teams; working with designers to create seamless mobile experiences for iOS and Android; defining and tracking product metrics including conversion rates, app engagement, and customer satisfaction; managing feature development through agile sprints; collaborating with data science teams to incorporate telematics and machine learning into product features; understanding insurance industry requirements and regulatory considerations; participating in customer feedback sessions and usability testing. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Business, Design, or related field; strong analytical skills and data-driven mindset; ability to analyze data using SQL or Excel; excellent communication and collaboration skills; interest in insurtech and mobile product development; understanding of mobile app user experience patterns; ability to work in a fast-paced startup environment; creative problem-solving and willingness to experiment. Preferred: experience with mobile app development or design; familiarity with insurance or financial services; experience with agile development tools like JIRA; understanding of data science concepts and how they can be applied to products.

Stripe builds economic infrastructure for the internet. As a New Grad Product Manager, you will help make online commerce more accessible by building payment products, financial tools, and platform features used by millions of businesses. Responsibilities include defining product requirements for payment processing, financial reporting, or developer platform features; working closely with engineering teams to build scalable, reliable financial infrastructure; analyzing transaction data, API usage patterns, and merchant feedback to inform product decisions; conducting user research with developers, business owners, and financial teams; writing technical product specifications that account for complex financial and regulatory requirements; designing and analyzing experiments to optimize payment flows and conversion rates; collaborating with design teams on user interfaces for dashboards, APIs, and developer documentation; understanding payment industry dynamics including card networks, banking regulations, and fraud prevention; partnering with sales and partnerships teams to understand merchant needs; managing the full product lifecycle from discovery through launch. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Economics, or related field; strong technical aptitude with the ability to understand APIs, system architecture, and engineering trade-offs; analytical skills with proficiency in SQL and data analysis; excellent written communication skills; interest in financial infrastructure and payments technology; comfort working with developer-focused products; ability to simplify complex technical and financial concepts; strong problem-solving and critical thinking skills. Preferred: software development experience or computer science coursework; experience with APIs and developer tools; understanding of payments or financial services; experience with product management internships or projects.

Cisco's APM program develops the next generation of technology product leaders. You will work on networking, security, or collaboration products that power the internet and connect organizations worldwide. Responsibilities include supporting the development of enterprise networking, cybersecurity, or collaboration products; conducting technical market research and competitive analysis in enterprise technology; gathering requirements from enterprise customers, IT administrators, and network engineers; writing product requirements documents and feature specifications; collaborating with engineering teams on product development using agile methodologies; analyzing product telemetry data to understand usage patterns and identify improvement opportunities; working with UX teams to improve the usability of complex enterprise interfaces; understanding network architecture, security protocols, and cloud infrastructure concepts; coordinating product launches with marketing, sales engineering, and customer success teams; contributing to pricing strategy and business model development for product lines. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business, or related field; technical foundation with understanding of networking concepts, cloud computing, or cybersecurity fundamentals; analytical skills with ability to interpret data; strong written and verbal communication skills; interest in enterprise technology and networking; ability to work with highly technical teams; project management skills and organizational abilities; willingness to learn complex technical domains quickly. Preferred: networking certifications or coursework in computer networks; experience with cloud platforms like AWS, Azure, or GCP; understanding of SaaS and subscription business models; internship experience in product management or engineering.

Toast is the all-in-one restaurant technology platform. As a Junior Product Manager, you will help build software that helps restaurants manage operations, payments, and customer engagement. Responsibilities include owning features within Toast's restaurant management platform including point-of-sale, online ordering, and kitchen display systems; conducting user research with restaurant owners, managers, servers, and kitchen staff; analyzing operational data to identify opportunities to streamline restaurant workflows; writing user stories and product requirements for engineering teams; collaborating with UX designers to create intuitive interfaces for busy restaurant environments; defining and tracking product metrics related to transaction processing, order accuracy, and customer satisfaction; managing feature development using agile sprints and scrum ceremonies; understanding the restaurant industry including point-of-sale systems, payment processing, and food service operations; gathering feedback from Toast's restaurant customers and field teams; coordinating with hardware teams on integrated payment and display solutions. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Business, Engineering, or related field; strong analytical and problem-solving skills; ability to work with data using SQL or Excel; excellent communication and interpersonal skills; interest in the restaurant and hospitality industry; understanding of software development processes; ability to empathize with diverse user personas from tech-savvy to non-technical; organizational skills to manage multiple priorities. Preferred: experience in the restaurant or hospitality industry; familiarity with payment processing systems; experience with agile development and product management tools; understanding of hardware-software integration.

Workday is a leader in enterprise cloud applications for finance and human resources. As an Associate Product Manager, you will help design and build HR technology and financial management solutions used by thousands of the world's largest organizations. Responsibilities include defining product requirements for HR management, payroll, or financial planning features; conducting user research with HR professionals, finance teams, and enterprise administrators; analyzing product usage data and customer feedback to prioritize improvements; collaborating with engineering teams to develop scalable enterprise cloud solutions; writing detailed product specifications and acceptance criteria; working with UX designers to create accessible, intuitive enterprise interfaces; understanding complex business processes in human resources, payroll, and financial management; managing feature development through agile sprints; coordinating with customer success and professional services teams to understand deployment needs; contributing to product roadmap planning and strategic initiatives. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Business, Engineering, Human Resources, or related field; strong analytical and quantitative skills; proficiency in data analysis using SQL or Excel; excellent written and verbal communication skills; interest in enterprise software, HR technology, or financial technology; ability to understand complex business workflows; collaborative mindset and ability to work across teams; attention to detail and strong organizational skills. Preferred: understanding of HR processes or financial management concepts; experience with enterprise SaaS products; familiarity with agile development methodologies; experience with data analysis or business intelligence tools.
